data.description: For example, if characler attempts climb a cliff, Dungeon Masler ask a character in task. Moreover, character help who lacks proficiency is for dangerous mighl for Athletics check. lf characler proficienl in two more is added to the Slrenglh check. lf characler that lacks lhe lhe proficiency, or just makes Slrenglh check.

Strength measures bodily power, athletic training, and the extent to which you can exert raw physical and force. Strength checks reflect an ability check of Strength (Athletics). They cover difficult situations such as climbing, jumping, swimming. Examples include long-distance running, or attempting to climb a sheer slippery wall, or cling to a surface while trying to knock something off. You might try to jump unusually far, or struggle against whirlpool-lashed waves, areas thick with seaweed, or storm-lost waves, or try to push or pull underwater an object or another creature in order to interfere with your own efforts. Some two more times or characters may attempt a Strength check to help a character leading the effort—or one who's highest modifier can make an ability check advantageous for that character.
